name: Dragon's Blood
description: Dragon's Blood is a vibrant red resin harvested from various plant species across the globe, most notably Dracaena, Daemonorops, and Croton trees. Its name evokes powerful imagery, connecting it to the mythical dragon and its potent life force. In numerous cultures, Dragon's Blood is revered for its protective, healing, and empowering properties. It is believed to amplify spells and rituals, banish negative energies, and attract love and prosperity. Shamans and healers traditionally utilize it for wound healing, stopping bleeding, and treating skin conditions. The deep red color is associated with blood, vitality, and courage, making it a symbol of strength and resilience. Its aroma is often described as earthy, spicy, and slightly sweet, contributing to its use in incense blends for spiritual cleansing and meditation. Dragon's Blood holds a significant place in folk magic, Wicca, and other spiritual practices. It is often incorporated into sachets, amulets, and talismans for protection and good fortune. The burning of Dragon's Blood incense is a common practice to purify spaces, ward off evil spirits, and enhance psychic abilities. Throughout history, it has been traded and valued for its medicinal and magical qualities, solidifying its status as a sacred and powerful herb across diverse cultures. The resin is more than just a substance; it is a conduit to ancient energies and a symbol of the dragon's enduring legacy. It represents the potent intersection of the natural and supernatural realms, embodying the life force and power associated with mythical dragons. Dragon's Blood is a reminder of the hidden energies within nature and the potential for harnessing them for healing, protection, and spiritual growth. Its continued use underscores its timeless appeal and enduring significance in various spiritual traditions.
subtitle: Resin of Power, Protection, and Vitality

keyMyths.title: The Origin of Dragon's Blood
keyMyths.description: Legend says Dragon's Blood sprung from the battle between a dragon and an elephant, where their mingled blood created the first tree. This tale signifies the herb's potent connection to life force and powerful energies.

sources.author: Scott Cunningham

extendedContent.title: Mythological Significance
extendedContent.content: The association of Dragon's Blood with dragons is deeply rooted in mythology and folklore. Dragons, often depicted as powerful and wise creatures, are symbols of potent life force, protection, and transformation. The red color of the resin further reinforces this connection, as red is frequently associated with blood, vitality, and strength. In various cultures, it was believed that Dragon's Blood contained the actual blood of dragons, imbued with their magical properties. This belief elevated the resin to a position of great importance in magical practices, where it was used to invoke the dragon's power and protection. The use of Dragon's Blood was seen as a way to tap into the ancient wisdom and primal energy of these mythical beasts, enhancing the efficacy of spells and rituals.

extendedContent.title: Magical Applications
extendedContent.content: Dragon's Blood holds a prominent place in various magical traditions. It is highly regarded for its protective properties, forming a shield against negative energies and malevolent entities. It is frequently used in rituals aimed at banishing unwanted influences and creating a sacred space. Dragon's Blood is also believed to amplify the power of spells and intentions, making it a valuable ingredient in rituals for love, prosperity, and healing. The resin is often burned as incense, its smoke carrying the practitioner's desires and intentions into the spiritual realm. In some traditions, Dragon's Blood is added to amulets and talismans to enhance their protective qualities. Its versatility and potent energy make it a staple in the arsenals of many magical practitioners across different cultures and belief systems.

extendedContent.title: Medicinal and Practical Uses
extendedContent.content: Beyond its magical applications, Dragon's Blood also boasts a history of medicinal and practical uses. Its astringent and antiseptic properties have made it a valuable remedy for treating wounds, cuts, and skin ailments. Historically, it was used to stop bleeding, promote healing, and protect against infection. In some cultures, it was also used to treat digestive issues and respiratory problems. Dragon's Blood has also found use as a dye and varnish. Its vibrant red color makes it a desirable pigment for coloring textiles, paints, and other materials. As a varnish, it provides a protective coating and adds a rich, reddish hue to wood and other surfaces. The diverse applications of Dragon's Blood highlight its versatility and enduring value throughout history.

habitat: Dragon's Blood resin is harvested from various tree species, most commonly found in Southeast Asia (Daemonorops), the Canary Islands (Dracaena draco), and South America (Croton). These trees thrive in tropical and subtropical climates.
shortDescription: Dragon's Blood is a red resin used globally in magic, medicine, & ritual. It's sourced from various plants and is believed to have potent protective properties.
uses: Amplifying the potency of spells and rituals
